title: GUIDELINES ON SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS

sections.section: GUIDELINES ON SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
sections.summary: Document title: Guidelines on Substances Used as Processing Aids.
Guidelines intended to provide information for the safe use of substances used as processing aids and for assessing the safety of their residues in foods and food ingredients.

Defines 'processing aid' as a non-consumed substance or material (not including apparatus/utensils) intentionally used during processing to fulfil a technological purpose and which may result in non-intentional but unavoidable residues or derivatives in the final product.

sections.section: 3. PRINCIPLES FOR THE SAFE USE OF SUBSTANCES USED AS PROCESSING AIDS
sections.summary: Sets principles: use of processing aids must be justified by a technological function; they must be used under GMP (minimise quantity and residues, handled as food ingredients); safety must be demonstrated including assessment of residues; substances should be food-grade per Codex or equivalent specifications; and they must meet applicable microbiological criteria and hygiene codes.

sections.section: 3.1 The use of a substance as a processing aid is justified when such use performs one or more technological functions during treatment or processing of raw materials, foods, or ingredients. Any residues of processing aids remaining in the food after processing should not perform a technological function in the final product.
sections.summary: A substance is justified as a processing aid only if it performs a technological function during processing, and any residues remaining should not perform a technological function in the final product.

sections.section: 3.2 Substances used as processing aids shall be used under conditions of good manufacturing practices (GMP) which includes the following:
sections.summary: Under GMP, processing aids must be used at the lowest achievable quantity necessary for their function, residues/derivatives must be reduced as far as reasonably achievable and not pose health risks, and the substance must be prepared and handled like a food ingredient.

sections.section: 3.3 The safety of a substance used as a processing aid should be demonstrated by the supplier or the user of the  substance.  The  demonstration  of  safety  should  include  appropriate  assessment  of  any  unintended  or unavoidable residues resulting from its use as a processing aid under conditions of GMP.
sections.summary: The supplier or user must demonstrate the safety of a processing aid, including appropriate assessment of any unintended or unavoidable residues resulting from its use under GMP.

sections.section: 3.4 Substances  used  as  processing  aids  should  be  of  food  grade  quality.  This  can  be  demonstrated  by conforming to the applicable specifications of identity and purity recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission  or,  in  the  absence  of  such  a  specification,  with  an  appropriate  specification  developed  by responsible national or international bodies or suppliers.
sections.summary: Processing aids should be of food-grade quality, demonstrated by conformity to Codex identity and purity specifications or, if none exist, to appropriate specifications developed by responsible national/international bodies or suppliers.

sections.section: 3.5 Substances  used  as  processing  aids  should  comply  with  any  applicable  microbiological  criteria established  in  accordance  with  the Principles  for  the  Establishment  and  Application  of  Microbiological Criteria  for  Foods (CAC/GL  21-1997)  and  should  be  prepared  and  handled  in  accordance  with  the Recommended International Code of Practice - General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts'
sections.summary: Processing aids should comply with applicable microbiological criteria (per CAC/GL 21-1997) and be prepared and handled in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts.
Labelling of substances used as processing aids should follow CODEX STAN 107-1981 (labelling of food additives sold as such) and CODEX STAN 1-1985 (labelling of prepackaged food).
